reviews.review_msg: Patrick manage his own firm. He is an excellent prof who provides students with practical experiences!

Patrick is one of the best professors at SFU for Finance. He has extensive industry experience through running his own boutique investment firm. He focuses helping students understand core and nececessary concepts and not just regurgitating the text. Strongly recommended for any student who wants to succeed in Finance, to learn from Patrick.

Patrick is very knowledgeable and easy-going. Gives sufficient feedback on exercises. He talks about many real life examples (e.g. projects he has done) to show how the textbook knowledge can be applied in real world. He cares about the analysis of financial data, which we need to know when go to work. definitely enjoyed his class.

Patrick is a great prof! He is one person who teaches important stuff related to the course with in depth knowledge of the way the industry works. He communicates information as if he is a friend of yours and not a teacher and he is always available for extra help after class. He gives great feedback and encourages the class to ask questions

I had the pleasure of having Patrick as my professor this semester. He really cares about his students and does a great job at teaching material that will help once you leave school and begin working. He isn't an academic and this is hugely important in finance - it made for an interesting and practical learning experience. I enjoyed his class!

Patrick is one of the most polarizing professors at SFU. He is not like a normal professor, as he does not have an academic background. Having said that, I really enjoyed this class. He is very knowledgeable and eager to help.  I think it is imperative to have a class like this, where you gain experience that is closer to a professional setting.

410 has been one of great classes. learn so much from lecture, he also from industry, so he has real world story and experience. and even group project is tough it is very fair. definitely recommended.

Patrick is a very patient professor. Despite the questions are easy or difficult, his explanations are detailed and useful. He will answer your questions if you reach him in the class or at office hours. Try not to reach him by email cuz he is too busy to reply. He is a easy marker and the class is so interesting if you are into stock investment.
Amazing lecturer who makes you think outside the it. It's not all about theory but putting it into practice.
I really enjoyed the course.  Great teacher although very demanding course.  Two essays, three quizzes.  A very international perspective.  Great lectures and interesting reading materials.

Patrick is a boss in real life. He is nice and enthusiastic. His 410 is fairly easy except he gives a lot of work: 3 quizzes, 2 papers, 3 writing assignments, 1 presentation (but no final). He is an easy marker and gives good grades. If you don't mind having lots of work, you should choose him for 410.
Always smiling. Encouraging. Enthusiastic.   Wonderful Professor.

One of the best teacher I've ever had at sfu. He was very helpful, he has the experience in really life business and you will learn a lot from him. quiz are more than fair if you read your slide. we did not have a final and the he graded assignment paper and report very fairly. I will recommend this teacher 100%
Good
He is bad. Avoid him. Learned nothing from him. his lecture and assignments are unclear.

WORST prof!!!!Avoid him!!!! His lectures takes full 4 hours and it's so boring. But he still expects you to follow him rather than falling asleep. If he finds you are not listening, he will ask you some weird questions. But actually no one can answer! He has no clear requirements on your team project. But he always think you should know it already!

Patrick is the best professor you will get in your finance concentration. Having worked in industry, I know this is EXACTLY what you want to learn. Patrick follows the markets and makes this apart of his lectures. At the same time, you will learn the ins and out of a company that are vital aspects of securing a position (internship or full time).
Worst prof I've had in SFU. Never respond to students. No rules and marking scheme for assignment and project. Avoid him at all your cost. Do not recommend him for any class.

Avoid him!!!!! Worst prof I ever had. He promised he would teach us practical skills  but he never did. He just expected us to learn by ourselves. Never replied emails. No clear requirement s for group project but obviously has some weird requirements that he would never let you know.

He thought that everyone shouldn't care about grade, but we do care about the grade as a student. He will treat u more like employees, but u r not his employees.  All assignments' requirements are vague. U will never reach him through email. Always forgot to send the lecture slides before the class. Never told u there is an assignment.

Patrick teaches you the fundamentals and a way of thinking rather than have you memorize formulas. Gives you freedom on putting what you think is important in an assignment rather than tell you point by point. This is good but also causes confusion A LOT. He's pretty disorganized overall but passionate lecturer.

For an upper division course, this is the type of professor you will want. Although he is not exactly the most organized professor, his passion and ability to bring the context of the course to the real world is incredibly valuable.  The paper is a strong capstone to the course, spend time on it and listen in lecture and you'll do fine.

This is simply the best course of my undergrad degree. If you are a finance major, I'd strongly recommend taking this class with Patrick. You will learn a lot. Focus on getting good group members, and actually understand the project. If you understand the project you'll do fine in the exams.
Great professor.  Very practical material. Enjoyed the class

extremely disappointing class, I was expecting to learn about Security analysis, wasnt the case. All he did was have us use excel, didnt explain anything about it and  expected us to finish it at home with no reference to how to complete the assignments. Seems like he made up the class as he went.

Patrick is a very nice guy. I have taken numerous Finance courses and the information learned in this class is the most valuable for me. He does not give outlines for reports which is great because you can focus on what you think is most importan. In fact, the reports I completed in this are the best reports I've ever been asked to do.
This is the greatest prof in SFU. His lecture is interesting and informative. The prof's class is practical and he will always tell you what happened in the real world.

I think for any higher level class, it is expected to know quite a bit of knowledge before even coming into class. Patrick is really nice and very helpful. The exam is not difficult and you can have good mark as long as you do all the work and also really being part of the project work.

Really frustrating prof. His lectures are so boring and pointless. He rambles on and on and gives only a five minute break. He gives no criteria for major projects leaving you high and dry. Quizzes are pretty straightforward but avoid this prof if you can!
